metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students explore advanced financial accounting principles and practices used by corporations to record, analyze, and report financial information. This course emphasizes Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), corporate financial statements, and the fundamental concepts of auditing.
metadata.general_description: Advanced financial accounting with focus on corporate statements, GAAP standards, and auditing concepts.

big_ideas.statement: Financial accounting systems translate business transactions into standardized information for decision-makers
big_ideas.elaboration: Accounting is the language of business, converting transactions into structured financial reports that stakeholders use to assess performance and make informed decisions.

big_ideas.statement: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) ensure consistency, reliability, and comparability of financial information
big_ideas.elaboration: GAAP standards provide a framework that allows financial statements to be interpreted consistently across organizations and time periods.

big_ideas.statement: Corporate financial statements present a comprehensive view of an organization's financial position, performance, and cash flows
big_ideas.elaboration: The balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow statement together provide stakeholders with insight into organizational health and sustainability.

big_ideas.statement: Auditing verifies the accuracy and integrity of financial information and strengthens stakeholder confidence
big_ideas.elaboration: Independent audits provide assurance that financial statements fairly represent an organization's financial condition and comply with accounting standards.

competencies.raw: Design accounting systems and processes that meet organizational needs
i_can_statements: I can identify the accounting information needs of different stakeholders
I can design a chart of accounts tailored to an organization's structure
I can develop accounting procedures that ensure accurate transaction recording
I can recommend accounting system improvements based on organizational goals

competencies.raw: Develop financial reports that communicate organizational performance effectively
i_can_statements: I can prepare comprehensive balance sheets with proper classifications
I can construct multi-step income statements that highlight key performance metrics
I can design supplementary financial schedules to support main statements
I can format financial reports for various stakeholder audiences

competencies.raw: Create audit procedures and controls to ensure financial integrity
i_can_statements: I can design internal controls to prevent and detect accounting errors
I can develop audit procedures to verify account balances
I can create audit documentation and working papers
I can identify areas of financial risk within an organization

competencies.raw: Plan accounting solutions for complex business scenarios
i_can_statements: I can analyze business transactions and determine appropriate accounting treatments
I can develop solutions for accounting challenges in different industries
I can plan accounting strategies that align with organizational objectives
I can adapt accounting processes for changing business circumstances

competencies.raw: Apply accounting principles and standards to record and classify transactions
i_can_statements: I can apply GAAP principles to determine proper accounting treatments
I can analyze and journalize complex business transactions
I can properly classify accounts using the chart of accounts
I can reconcile accounts to ensure accuracy

competencies.raw: Perform accounting adjustments and period-end closing procedures
i_can_statements: I can identify accounts requiring adjusting entries at period end
I can calculate and record depreciation, accruals, and prepayments
I can prepare trial balances and identify discrepancies
I can execute closing procedures to prepare for the next accounting period

competencies.raw: Analyze financial statements to assess organizational performance
i_can_statements: I can calculate and interpret common financial ratios
I can analyze trends in financial statements over multiple periods
I can assess liquidity, profitability, and solvency
I can identify factors affecting financial performance

competencies.raw: Execute audit procedures and evaluate control effectiveness
i_can_statements: I can perform substantive audit testing on account balances
I can evaluate the effectiveness of internal controls
I can document audit findings and recommendations
I can assess compliance with accounting standards and regulations

competencies.raw: Use accounting software to record, process, and report financial transactions
i_can_statements: I can navigate and configure accounting software systems
I can input and process transactions within accounting applications
I can generate financial reports from accounting software
I can troubleshoot common software issues and data entry errors

competencies.raw: Utilize spreadsheet tools to analyze and present financial data
i_can_statements: I can create financial models and scenarios using spreadsheets
I can use advanced formulas for financial calculations
I can develop dashboards to visualize accounting metrics
I can export and integrate data from multiple sources

competencies.raw: Apply digital tools to enhance accounting accuracy and efficiency
i_can_statements: I can use digital reconciliation tools to verify accounts
I can leverage automation to streamline repetitive tasks
I can implement cloud-based accounting solutions
I can protect accounting data through proper security practices

competencies.raw: Employ technology to communicate financial information effectively
i_can_statements: I can create professional financial reports and presentations
I can visualize complex accounting data through charts and graphs
I can format documents for different distribution methods
I can ensure digital accessibility of accounting information

areas.name: Advanced Transaction Recording and Journal Entries
areas.raw: Advanced Transaction Recording and Journal Entries
topics: Complex transactions in multiple currency and jurisdictions
Journal entries for partnerships and corporations
Elimination entries and consolidation concepts
Reversing entries and their applications
Analyzing and interpreting journal entries for internal control

areas.name: Corporate Financial Statements and GAAP Standards
areas.raw: Corporate Financial Statements and GAAP Standards
topics: Balance sheet presentation and asset/liability classifications
Income statement formats and revenue recognition principles
Statement of changes in equity and shareholders' equity
Cash flow statement preparation and analysis
Notes to financial statements and disclosure requirements

areas.name: Financial Analysis and Interpretation
topics: Liquidity ratios and working capital analysis
Profitability ratios and return on investment
Leverage ratios and capital structure analysis
Horizontal and vertical analysis of financial statements
Industry benchmarking and competitive analysis

areas.name: Auditing Principles and Practices
topics: Auditor responsibilities and professional standards
Internal controls framework and effectiveness evaluation
Audit procedures: sampling, testing, and verification
Audit documentation and working papers
Audit reporting and management letters
